ideas.title: Field Day Is Here — How to Actually Have Fun and Not Just Survive It
ideas.why_now: ARRL Field Day is June 27-28; multiple posts show operators actively prepping, debugging gear, and recruiting participants right now.
ideas.angle: Skip the pressure-cooker club politics and focus on what makes Field Day genuinely fun for everyday ops — portable setup tips, logging basics, and how to jump in even if your club isn't organized. Practical prep, not trophy hunting.

sources.label: Mastodon @W6KME IC-7410 laptop driver battle for Field Day
Mastodon @keepmunseeweird Field Day public event post

ideas.title: The Magic Band Is Open — Your Beginner's Guide to 6 Meters in Summer
ideas.why_now: Multiple operators reporting active sporadic-E openings on 6m this week, right at the summer solstice peak for the phenomenon.
ideas.angle: Six meters mystifies a lot of newer hams — explain what sporadic-E actually is in plain language, how to know when a band opening is happening, and what even a modest station can work during summer. Low barrier, high excitement.

sources.label: Mastodon @n8dmt 6m indoor antenna working East Coast

ideas.title: Ham Radio's Gendered Language Problem — Is It Time to Retire OM, YL, and 88?
ideas.why_now: A Mastodon thread calling for a new code of conduct to drop Victorian-era terms sparked real back-and-forth discussion this week.
ideas.angle: This is the kind of conversation everyday hams encounter but rarely address head-on. Cover the history of the terms, why some ops find them exclusionary, and what more welcoming on-air habits look like — without being preachy.

sources.label: Mastodon @dj3ei code of conduct thread, 6 comments
Mastodon @dj3ei 1928 code of conduct critique, 2 comments

ideas.title: When the Internet Dies, Does Your DMR Die With It? Internet-Linked Modes Explained
ideas.why_now: A pointed post this week calling out DMR's internet dependency sparked nods of recognition across the community.
ideas.angle: Walk through which popular modes — DMR, Fusion, D-STAR, EchoLink — actually require internet and which work standalone. Help everyday hams understand what they're really buying into before an emergency reveals the gap.

sources.label: Mastodon @ai6yr DMR internet dependency quip, 3 comments
QRZ Forums: How relevant is Ham Radio in disaster preparedness today?

ideas.title: AI-Generated QSL Cards and Automated FT8 — Where's the Line?
ideas.why_now: A post this week called out QSLworld.com for AI-generated cards and fully automated FT8 software, touching a live nerve about authenticity.
ideas.angle: Use this as a jumping-off point for a genuinely fun debate: what counts as 'you' making a contact? Automated logging vs. automated operating vs. AI artwork — where do everyday hams actually draw the line, and does it matter?

sources.label: Mastodon @croyle AI QSL cards and automated FT8 callout, 2 comments
Mastodon @es0mhi AI-generated CQ voice at Estonian Hamfest, 2 comments

ideas.title: 13 Colonies Special Event Is Coming — Here's How to Work It
ideas.why_now: The 13 Colonies Special Event runs July 1-7, making this the perfect pre-event explainer for listeners who've never tried a coordinated special event activation.
ideas.angle: Break down exactly how to find and work the 13 Colonies stations, what gear and bands to use, and why chasing special events is one of the most beginner-accessible ways to make memorable contacts. Practical and motivating.

sources.label: QRZ Forums: 13 Colonies Special Event July 1-7

ideas.title: Free-Range Portable: Operating Anywhere Without POTA or SOTA Rules
ideas.why_now: A QRZ thread this week floated the idea of portable operating completely outside any structured program, tapping into summer portable season energy.
ideas.angle: Not everyone wants to chase reference numbers — explore the joy of just grabbing a radio and heading outside with zero agenda. Cover kit choices, frequency planning, and why unstructured portable ops can be the most relaxing ham radio you'll do all summer.

sources.label: QRZ Forums: New portable operating idea — no parks, no summits, no rules
Mastodon @ai6yr failed POTA activation at El Presidio
Ham Radio Outside the Box: Road trip with VP2E antenna and wolves

ideas.title: Coax: The Part of Your Station You're Probably Neglecting
ideas.why_now: A relatable post this week about decade-old RadioShack coax finally failing — with a revealing look inside the shield — got instant community recognition.
ideas.angle: Turn a funny coax failure story into a practical guide: how to spot bad coax, what the actual differences between budget and quality feedline mean for your signal, and a simple testing routine any ham can do before Field Day or a portable activation.

sources.label: Mastodon @ai6yr RadioShack coax failure and autopsy, 3 comments
